summary: High‑quality soundboard capture of a fiery 1991 Greensboro show, highlighted by an epic Eyes of the World → Playin' sequence and soaring Jerry tones.
review: The recording delivers clear, well‑separated sound that lets you hear each member—Bob in the left channel, Hornsby’s piano front‑and‑center, and the dual‑guitar interplay. Musically the night shines with a monster Eyes of the World that rolls into a marathon Playin' jam, peppered with Dark Star licks, while the first set builds from a tentative start to solid grooves. A few mix hiccups (Samson & Delilah) and a shaky opening don’t detract from an overall vibrant performance.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers give 4‑5 stars, praising both the musical flow (Eyes → Playin' → Dark Star) and the clear soundboard mix; only a few note minor mix or performance glitches.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp stereo separation (Bob highlighted in left channel), strong presence of keyboards and guitars, occasional early‑set muddiness and a poorly mixed Samson & Delilah segment.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set starts tentative but gains momentum after four songs; second set explodes with the 23‑minute Eyes of the World that flows into a 38‑minute Playin' jam and Dark Star teases, delivering the night’s high point.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Soaring, midi‑enhanced guitar tone praised throughout; especially strong during the Eyes/Playin'/Dark Star passages.
band_member_comments.Phil: Described as “mega‑toned” and “quaking away,” providing a solid, energetic bass foundation under rapid drum turnarounds.
band_member_comments.Bob: Left‑channel placement highlighted his excellent rhythm‑guitar work; listeners note clear presence.
band_member_comments.Keys: Hornsby receives abundant praise for piano virtuosity, heavy midi textures, and creative contributions on Eyes, Playin' and the jam sections.
band_member_comments.Drums: Rapid turnabouts and dynamic fills praised early on, though a few reviewers felt the drummers “dozed off” later in the set.
